rationale: Postcondition requires post1_self_ == post2_self_ on HashMap<V>, but HashMap is an external_body struct (#[verifier(external_body)]) without #[verifier::ext_equal] and its only public spec is `uninterp spec fn view`. The contract for `insert` only gives `self@ == old(self)@.insert(key@, value)`, i.e. view equality. Verus encodes external_body types as opaque SMT sorts, so view equality cannot imply structural ==. Confirmed by a minimal repro: forall a,b: ExtBody, a@ == b@ ==> a == b is rejected even with #[verifier::ext_equal] added, because view is uninterpreted. The g_neq_tuple branch additionally assumes !det_insert_equal, making the obligation contradictory only under extensionality, which is unavailable. No valid Verus statement (no axioms, no assume) can discharge the goal without adding ext_equal to HashMap or strengthening insert's postcondition, which is outside the proof body and would not propagate through re-injection.
